items.original_text: Pre-finetuning involves a massive multi-task learning step (4.8 million total training examples) performed on around 50 classification, summarization, question answering, and common sense reasoning tasks.
items.claim_text: MUPPET 提出 pre-finetuning，在约50个数据集、480万标注样本上进行大规模多任务学习。

items.original_text: Pre-finetuning involves a massive multi-task learning step (4.8 million total training examples) performed on around 50 classification, summarization, question answering, and common sense reasoning tasks.
items.claim_text: MUPPET 提出 pre-finetuning，在约50个数据集、480万标注样本上进行大规模多任务学习。

items.original_text: Pre-finetuning involves a massive multi-task learning step (4.8 million total training examples) performed on around 50 classification, summarization, question answering, and common sense reasoning tasks.
items.claim_text: MUPPET proposes pre-finetuning: 4.8M examples across 46 datasets covering classification, summarization, QA, and commonsense reasoning.

items.original_text: Pre-finetuning involves a massive multi-task learning step (4.8 million total training examples) performed on around 50 classification, summarization, question answering, and common sense reasoning tasks.

We show that standard multi-tasking schemes can be unstable and often fail to learn high quality representations. However, we introduce a new training scheme which uses loss scaling and task-heterogeneous batches so that gradient steps are more evenly balanced across multiple different competing tasks.
We empirically see a critical point in terms of the number of tasks (usually over 15); having fewer tasks degrades representations, while having more seems to improve performance linearly.
items.claim_text: Critical point: ~15 tasks; linear improvement beyond.

items.original_text: Incorporating pre-finetuning to RoBERTa and BART models yields consistent improvements, including new state-of-the-art performance for RTE and HellaSWAG, particularly strong in the low resource regime.
items.claim_text: SOTA on RTE and HellaSWAG, especially low-resource.

items.original_text: Classification 26 datasets 2.9M train; Summarization 4 datasets 524K train; MRC 6 datasets 1.05M train; CommonSense 10 datasets 360K train; Total 46 datasets 4.8M train.
scaling up language models greatly improves task-agnostic, few-shot performance, sometimes even reaching competitiveness with prior state-of-the-art fine-tuning approaches
items.claim_text: GPT-3 (175B) shows that scaling up LM improves few-shot performance

items.original_text: For all tasks, GPT-3 is applied without any gradient updates or fine-tuning, with tasks and few-shot demonstrations specified purely via text interaction with the model.
items.claim_text: GPT-3 applies without gradient updates or fine-tuning, using only text interaction

items.original_text: Feedback Transformer architecture exposes all previous representations to all future representations, meaning the lowest representation of the current timestep is formed from the highest-level abstract representation of the past.
items.claim_text: Feedback Transformer exposes all previous representations to future, enabling shallow models to outperform comparable Transformers

items.original_text: even with residual connection and layer normalization, deep Transformers still have difficulty in training, and particularly Transformer models with more than 12 encoder/decoder layers fail to converge
items.claim_text: Deep Transformers (>12 layers) fail to converge even with residual connections and layer normalization

items.original_text: GPT-3 trains an autoregressive language model with 175 billion parameters, 10x more than any previous non-sparse language model, and tests its performance in the few-shot setting.
items.claim_text: GPT-3 (175B parameters) is 10x larger than any previous non-sparse language model

items.original_text: During unsupervised pre-training, a language model develops a broad set of skills and pattern recognition abilities. It then uses these abilities at inference time to rapidly adapt to or recognize the desired task. We use the term in-context learning to describe the inner loop of this process.
items.claim_text: GPT-3 uses in-context learning - skills developed during pre-training are applied at inference time via text interaction
